briefing.prompt: Student has been working on Chopin Nocturne Op. 9 No. 2. MuQ shows consistent dynamic deviation in measures 5-8 — playing forte where the score marks piano. Student says: 'I keep getting too loud in that middle section. Can you give me something specific to practice to fix this?'

Student is learning Bach Prelude in C major, BWV 846. MuQ detects rushed sixteenth notes in measures 3-6 with timing variance of 18ms above threshold. Student says: 'My fingers feel uneven. I want a drill to fix the rhythm in that passage.'

Student is practicing Beethoven Sonata Op. 13 'Pathetique' first movement. MuQ flags heavy pedaling throughout — sustain pedal held for full bars where score marks quarter-note pedal. Student says: 'Can you design a pedaling exercise for me? I want to practice lifting the pedal at the right moments.'

Student is working on Debussy Clair de Lune. MuQ shows articulation issues in the triplet passage at measure 27 — notes are clipped short, staccato instead of legato. Student says: 'I struggle to connect those triplets smoothly. Please make me an exercise for legato triplets.'

Student is practicing Mozart Sonata K. 331 first movement. MuQ detects dynamic inconsistency specifically in the second variation — forte markings underplayed. Teacher wants to draw the student's attention to measures 17-24 in the score where the dynamic contrast is most critical.

Student is working on Schubert Impromptu Op. 90 No. 3. MuQ flags pedaling overlap creating harmonic muddiness in measures 10-14. The score shows explicit pedal markings at those bars that the student is ignoring. Teacher wants to highlight those measure markings on the score.

Student is learning Chopin Ballade No. 1. MuQ analysis shows phrasing cutoffs too early — phrases end at measure boundaries instead of at the written slur endings. Teacher wants to mark the slur arcs spanning measures 30-36 on the score to guide the student.

Student is practicing Beethoven Sonata Op. 27 No. 2 'Moonlight' first movement. MuQ detects accent placement errors — beats 2 and 3 receiving stress when the triplet figure requires even dynamics. Teacher wants to annotate the score at measures 5-8 showing where not to accent.

Student is a beginner working on hand position for octave passages in Beethoven Sonata Op. 2 No. 1. Student says: 'My hand cramps when I play octaves. Can you show me on the keyboard where my fingers should land for these stretches?'

Student is learning Chopin Etude Op. 10 No. 1 for the first time. The wide arpeggio stretches of a tenth are new to them. Student says: 'I cannot figure out the hand shape for those wide arpeggios. Can you show me the keyboard positions?'

Student is practicing Bach Invention No. 1 and struggling with fingering in the left hand crossing passage at measure 11. Student says: 'My left hand gets tangled when the thumb has to cross under. Can you show me where each finger lands on the keys?'

Student has just finished a 30-minute practice session on Debussy Arabesque No. 1. Student says: 'How did I do today overall? Can you show me the scores from this session?' Teacher has MuQ data for the full session.

Student is concerned about whether their dynamics have improved over the past few sessions on Brahms Intermezzo Op. 118 No. 2. Student asks: 'Has my dynamic range been getting better? Can I see the numbers from today?'

Student practicing Rachmaninoff Prelude Op. 23 No. 5 asks to review the timing scores from the current session before they stop for the day. Student says: 'Before I quit, can you pull up the timing data for what I played today?'

Student asks about the historical context of Chopin's Nocturnes while working on Op. 9 No. 1. Student says: 'What was the musical era that influenced Chopin's nocturne style? Can you look that up for me?' Teacher wants to fetch a reference article on the Romantic period nocturne tradition.

Student is studying the Baroque ornamentation in Bach French Suite No. 5. Student asks: 'I don't understand trills in Baroque music. Can you find me a clear explanation of how to execute them correctly?' Teacher wants to fetch a reference on Baroque ornament practice.

Student is preparing a recital piece, Scarlatti Sonata K. 466, and wants to understand the harpsichord performance tradition. Student says: 'How should this piece be adapted for modern piano from harpsichord? Is there reference material on this?' Teacher wants to look up a scholarly article on Scarlatti piano adaptation.

Student has mastered Hanon exercises and wants to move up to something more musical but still focused on technique. Student says: 'I am done with Hanon and want a real piece to practice scales. What do you recommend for intermediate technique building?' Teacher wants to search the repertoire catalog for suitable etudes.

Student is at an advanced intermediate level and wants to explore Romantic-period character pieces. Student says: 'I have finished Kuhlau sonatinas. What should I learn next — something expressive but not too long?' Teacher wants to search the catalog for appropriate Romantic character pieces.

Adult beginner student wants to play a recognizable classical piece at their first recital in 6 months. Student says: 'I want something famous that people will recognize but is achievable for a beginner. What pieces are in the catalog at that level?' Teacher wants to search catalog for beginner-friendly well-known classical pieces.
Student just finished their practice session. Student says: 'Thanks for the feedback last week! It really helped. By the way, do you know if there are any good piano concerts coming up in the city?'
Student is chatting before starting their practice. Student says: 'I have been really tired lately because of work. How do you stay motivated to practice when you are exhausted?'
Student asks after a session: 'Do you think Chopin or Liszt was the better composer? I have always wondered about that debate.'
Student says: 'My cat kept jumping on the keys while I was trying to practice. Have you ever heard of animals being trained to play piano?'

Student has just sat down at the piano and has not yet played anything. Student says: 'I am going to practice the Moonlight Sonata today. What exercises should I do?' No MuQ data is available yet for the current session.
Student logs in and says: 'I want to work on Debussy today.' No session data has been recorded yet. Teacher has no MuQ analysis to base recommendations on.
Student opens the app and immediately asks: 'Can you highlight the problem spots in my Schubert piece?' The student has not played anything yet and no MuQ analysis exists for this session.

Student has just started a new piece — Beethoven Bagatelle Op. 126 No. 4 — and played only the first four bars. Student asks: 'What is my biggest weakness in this piece?' Insufficient data for a reliable tool call.

Student says: 'Can you help me with my phrasing?' No MuQ data has been collected yet, no piece has been specified, and it is unclear whether they want an exercise, a score annotation, or a conceptual explanation.

Student says after a short session: 'I feel like something is off in my playing. Can you look into it?' MuQ detected minor deviations across dynamics, timing, and pedaling with roughly equal severity. It is unclear which dimension to focus on or what action is most appropriate.

Student asks: 'Can you show me the problem in the Chopin piece?' There are two Chopin pieces in the session log — Op. 10 No. 3 and Op. 25 No. 1 — and MuQ flagged issues in both. The student has not specified which piece or which type of problem.

Student is still in the same session. MuQ flagged dynamics in measures 12-15 of the Mozart Sonata K. 545. Teacher already called create_exercise 8 minutes ago and generated a dynamic shaping drill for exactly those bars. Student has not yet practiced that drill. Recommending another exercise now would be redundant.

Teacher called search_catalog 5 minutes ago and returned three appropriate etude suggestions (Czerny Op. 299 Nos. 1, 3, and 7) for the student's technique goals. The student has not responded yet. Calling search_catalog again for the same goal would return duplicate results.

Teacher called score_highlight 10 minutes ago to mark the pedal markings in measures 10-14 of the Schubert Impromptu. Student acknowledged them. The student is still struggling with the same passage but the score is already annotated — highlighting the same bars again adds no new information.

Student says: 'I am also learning guitar on the side. Can you recommend some guitar exercises for fingerpicking coordination? I figure the technique might transfer.' This request is outside the piano practice domain entirely.

Student asks: 'Can you help me write a short essay about Beethoven for my music history class? I have to submit it tomorrow.' This is an academic writing request unrelated to piano practice or performance feedback.

Student asks: 'What is the best piano keyboard to buy under $500 for a beginner? I want to upgrade from my current one.' This is a product purchasing recommendation request outside the scope of the teacher system's practice tools.

MuQ shows that the student's dynamics are weak in the Brahms Intermezzo. Student asks: 'Can you show me which notes in the score I should be playing louder?' The teacher is tempted to call score_highlight but the root issue is a motor-skill dynamic control problem that requires create_exercise, not a visual annotation.

Student asks: 'I want to find a piece with a lot of pedal work to practice. Can you search for something?' Teacher considers search_catalog but the student actually has three suitable pieces already in the session history that have not been addressed. Searching the catalog before exhausting the current repertoire is the wrong action.

Student is struggling with the hand crossing in a Bach Invention. Teacher considers keyboard_guide to show the positions, but MuQ data clearly shows the problem is rushing — a timing issue, not a positional one. A keyboard visualization would not address the actual root cause identified by the analysis.
